file_name: SLOVAKWIRELESSFINANCECOBV_03_28_2001-EX-4.(B)(II).3-Maintenance and support contract for SICAP(R) modules.txt

perturbation.original_text: EuroTel defines up to 5 system administrators from their own staff. They must be trained for the following modules: o SICAP(R)VCA o SICAP(R)PPB o SICAP(R)OTA Only these system administrators have the permission to use the HotLine and the "GNATS tool".
perturbation.changed_text: EuroTel defines up to 5 system administrators from their own staff. They must be trained for at least one of the following modules: o SICAP(R)VCA o SICAP(R)PPB o SICAP(R)OTA All Eurotel staff are permitted to use the HotLine and the "GNATS tool".
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces conflicting requirements regarding system administrators. Initially, only trained administrators on all modules had access to the Hotline. Now, access is granted to all Eurotel staff, regardless of training or administrative role. This creates a contradiction on who is authorized to request support, potentially overwhelming Sicap with unqualified requests and creating confusion on the support process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: According to the priority request of EuroTel, ISC SICAP(R) (Installation and Service Center) will contact the responsible engineer from Sicap Ltd with its Subcontractor(s) immediately. The following procedures are applicable: 1. After the discovery of an incident, ISC SICAP(R) has to be notified without delay by the Web based "GNATS trouble ticket tool" and by phone (called HotLine). [LOGO] sicap M&S contract -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 2. EuroTel shall use the HotLine number to report every incident. Together with notification of the HotLine the following information has to be provided: o Date and time of incident occurrence o User domain, site domain, application domain o Configuration item detail and a full and accurate description of the incident o Priority of Incident 3. On request from Sicap Ltd, EuroTel has to provide the following supplementary information: o SICAP(R) modules VCA- or PPB- or OTA-logfile o System logfiles o all necessary information from the crashed system in electronic way (if possible ) like memory dumps. 4. On request from Sicap Ltd, EuroTel has to open the ISDN remote access to the system to ISC SICAP(R)
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts Hotline and 'GNATS tool' access to trained system administrators, while the changed text expands access to all Eurotel staff. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which specifies that EuroTel, through ISC SICAP(R), must notify Sicap Ltd of incidents using the 'GNATS trouble ticket tool' and the HotLine, and provide supplementary information (SICAP(R) modules VCA- or PPB- or OTA-logfile, system logfiles). If all Eurotel staff can use the hotline/tool, regardless of training, the quality of incident reports and the provided supplementary information may degrade, potentially overwhelming Sicap and hindering effective support, violating the implicit requirement in the contradicted text that EuroTel provide qualified notifications. This creates ambiguity and a strong in-text contradiction regarding who is authorized to request support and provide necessary technical information.

perturbation.original_text: In an emergency case Sicap Ltd shall do every effort to make the SICAP(R) application running within 24 hours after response. This also includes to find temporary solution (work around) until the definitive acceptable solution is implemented.
perturbation.changed_text: In an emergency case Sicap Ltd aims to make the SICAP(R) application running within a reasonable time after response, however, there is no obligation for it to be solved in 24 hours. This also includes to find temporary solution (work around) until the definitive acceptable solution is implemented.
perturbation.explanation: The original text stated a 24-hour target for resolving emergency cases, while the changed text removes this commitment, making the timeframe subjective. This introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation of Sicap Ltd in emergency situations. This change directly contradicts the response times outlined in section 3.1, creating uncertainty about service level agreements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Response Time during the HotLine availability hours shall be the following: -------------------------------------------------------------------------- Priority of reques Availability Response Time -------------------------------------------------------------------------- emergency 24 hours, 7 days a week 4 hours -------------------------------------------------------------------------- high during working hours 4 hours -------------------------------------------------------------------------- medium during working hours next working day -------------------------------------------------------------------------- low during working hours day after next working day -------------------------------------------------------------------------- It is up to EuroTel to decide which priority occurs. In case of an obvious misuse, Sicap Ltd has the right to charge the costs. Sicap Ltd will perform every possibility to make the system running as soon as possible. Staff of EuroTel must be on-site on request of Sicap Ltd.
perturbation.justification: The original text set a specific 24-hour target for resolving emergency cases. The changed text removes this concrete commitment, replacing it with 'a reasonable time' and explicitly stating 'there is no obligation for it to be solved in 24 hours'. This directly contradicts the 'Response Time' table in the contradicted text, which states 'emergency 24 hours, 7 days a week 4 hours'. The change introduces ambiguity and weakens the previously defined obligation, creating a clear conflict and making the response time for emergencies uncertain and unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: After placing an order based on Sicap Ltd's quotation, Sicap Ltd implements the change according to the agreed schedule.
perturbation.changed_text: After placing an order based on Sicap Ltd's quotation, Sicap Ltd will attempt to implements the change according to the agreed schedule.
perturbation.explanation: The original text indicates that, after order being placed for a quotation, the implementation is a must according to the schedule agreed by both sides. The modification in the text indicates that Sicap, once the quotation order is placed and the implementation according to the schedule agreed will be just an intention to do it, so is not obligatory that Sicap is going to implement such implementations in time and form agreed with Eurotel. This creates a conflict with section 4.1 where the responsibility is of Sicap Ltd to coordinate and communicate the progress in weekly basis.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Under Change Management, Sicap Ltd has the responsibility to: o collect and assess RFC's, resulting in a quotation and a schedule to EuroTel o co-ordinate and communicate about progress on a weekly basis. EuroTel has the responsibility to: o accept or decline the quotation and the delivery plan o accept the implemented change
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Sicap Ltd *implements* the change according to the agreed schedule, establishing a firm obligation. The changed text modifies this to 'will attempt to implements the change,' weakening the commitment to a mere intention. This directly contradicts the Contradicted Text, where Sicap Ltd has the *responsibility* to coordinate and communicate about progress on a weekly basis, implying a mandatory commitment to implement the changes as agreed. By changing 'implements' to 'will attempt to implement,' the obligation on Sicap is lessened and creates a potential conflict with their responsibilities stated in the Contradicted Text.
